data.title: Gasoline cracks slip on rising US stocks
data.description:
Northwest European gasoline profit margins fell sharply on Thursday, dropping by more than $3 to $19.55 a barrel on higher U.S. inventories.

  • A total of 7,000 metric tons of Eurobob E5 gasoline barges traded as Gunvor sold to TotalEnergies and MB Energy.

  • Another 4,000 tons of Eurobob E10 gasoline barges traded, with Gunvor selling to Equinor.

  • Gasoline stocks held independently in the Amsterdam-Rotterdam-Antwerp (ARA) refining and storage hub fell slightly to 975,000 metric tons on the week, data from Dutch consultancy Insights Global showed.

  • U.S. gasoline stocks (USOILG=ECI) rose by 2.3 million barrels last week to 207.4 million barrels, the Energy Information Administration said, against the draw of 200,000 barrels expected by analysts in a Reuters poll.​

  • Meanwhile, EU and UK gasoline exports are on track to reach 790,000 barrels per day in November, Kpler data shows, compared with 916,000 bpd last month. ​

data.title: FedEx Is Maintained at Buy by Stifel
CHI FED'S GOOLSBEE Q&A/CFA: HAVE SEEN 'NOTABLE DOWNTURN' IN CREATION OF NEW JOBS OVER LAST FEW MOS; IMMIGRATION CUTBACKS NOT RECESSION SIGNAL
Fed's Goolsbee repeats he is uneasy on rate cuts
data.description:
Chicago Federal Reserve President Austan Goolsbee repeated on Thursday he is "uneasy" about frontloading interest-rate cuts, particularly with progress on inflation towards the Fed's 2% goal looking to have stalled and starting to go the wrong way.

"In my view, this underlying the economy is pretty strong and I feel like eventually we are going to be back to, rates can come down a fair amount, but in the near term I'm a little uneasy front loading too many rate cuts, and counting on (that) this will be transitory and inflation will go back down," Goolsbee told the Chartered Financial Analyst Society of Indianapolis.

The lack of official data due to the government shutdown, he said, makes him even more uneasy because without it the Fed does not have as much visibility into price pressures as it does on the state of the labor market.

data.title: Genspark Raises $200M in Series B Funding Round
data.description:
By Stephen Nakrosis

Genspark raised $200 million in a Series B financing round which values the agentic AI company at $1.25 billion.

The round was backed by Emergence Capital, SBI Investment, LG Technology Ventures, Pavilion Capital and Uphonest Capital, Genspark said. It also included participation from all existing investors, Genspark added.

The funding round coincides with the launch of the Genspark AI Workspace, which selects and orchestrates from numerous AI models to deliver a final product, the company said.

"Our platform enables knowledge workers to focus on strategy and decisions while autonomous agents handle execution. You state your intent, we deliver the finished work," Eric Jing, the company's chief executive officer, said.

Genspark also achieved $50 million in annualized run rate within five months of its initial launch, it said.

Write to Stephen Nakrosis at stephen.nakrosis@wsj.com

data.title: Merck's $3 Billion AI Gamble: Inside the Bold Bet to Crack Parkinson's
data.description:
Merck KGaA is making a fresh push to rebuild its pharmaceutical firepower, and this time it is placing a multibillion-dollar bet on artificial intelligence. The company has signed a new partnership with Valo Health that could be worth more than $3 billion, handing Merck access to an AI platform trained on over 17 million patient records and biobank samples. The goal is straightforward but ambitious: identify disease patterns and potential targets for Parkinson's and related disorders at a moment when Merck could be looking to regain momentum after two pipeline setbacks and increasing competitive pressure on some older products.

For Valo, the deal adds another heavyweight name to a partnership list that already includes Novo Nordisk, reinforcing how its AI-driven approach might be gaining traction across the industry. The agreement announced Thursday bundles upfront payments, potential milestones, royalties, and R&D funding, though the companies did not break down the financials. While Valo continues to advance its own experimental programs, collaborations like this could be a way for the company to scale its platform more quickly.

This latest move also lands as CEO Belen Garijo prepares to step down in May, after pushing through a sequence of transactions aimed at strengthening Merck's pipeline. The recently acquired SpringWorks Therapeutics has already begun contributing to sales, and an August agreement with Skyhawk Therapeutics is targeting neurological conditions with high unmet need. With Mavenclad, one of Merck's key multiple sclerosis growth drivers, now facing the possibility of earlier-than-expected generic competition following a US patent blow, this new AI-enabled partnership could be an important piece of Merck's longer-term strategy to rebuild momentum in neurology.

data.title: Fitch to Rate BX Trust 2025-DELC; Presale Issued
data.description:
Fitch Ratings-Chicago-20 November 2025:

Fitch Ratings has assigned the following expected ratings and Outlooks to BX Trust 2025-DELC, Commercial Mortgage Pass-Through Certificates, Series 2025-DELC.

BX 2025-DELC

----A ; Long Term Rating; Expected Rating; AAA(EXP)sf; Rating Outlook Stable

----B ; Long Term Rating; Expected Rating; AA-(EXP)sf; Rating Outlook Stable

----C ; Long Term Rating; Expected Rating; NR(EXP)sf

----D ; Long Term Rating; Expected Rating; NR(EXP)sf

----E ; Long Term Rating; Expected Rating; NR(EXP)sf

----F ; Long Term Rating; Expected Rating; NR(EXP)sf

----HRR ; Long Term Rating; Expected Rating; NR(EXP)sf

Fitch expects to rate the transaction and assign Outlooks as follows:

--$382,100,000 Class A 'AAA(EXP)sf'; Outlook Stable;

--$72,400,000 Class B 'AA-(EXP)sf'; Outlook Stable.

Fitch does not expect to rate the following classes:

--$154,100,000 Class C;

--$106,200,000 Class D;

--$74,700,000 Class E;

--$132,000,000 Class F;

--$48,500,000a Class HRR.

(a) Horizonal credit risk retention interest.

Transaction Summary

The certificates represent the beneficial interest in a trust that is expected to hold a $970 million, two-year, floating-rate, IO mortgage loan with three one-year extension options. The mortgage will be secured by the borrowers' fee simple and leasehold interests in Hotel Del Coronado, a historic, full-service, 938-key luxury beachfront resort situated on Coronado Island in San Diego, CA. The hotel includes a total of 938 keys, including 718 collateral keys, and 220 rooms comprising the condominium-hotel units at Beach Village and Shore House, which are individually owned residences managed by the borrowers under a revenue-sharing agreement.

Mortgage loan proceeds will be used to refinance $950 million in prior debt and pay an estimated $20 million in closing-related costs. The loan's sponsor is Blackstone Real Estate Partners VIII L.P., an affiliate of Blackstone Inc. The sponsor reportedly maintains approximately $630 million in cash equity in the property.

The loan is expected to be co-originated by Goldman Sachs Bank USA, Morgan Stanley Bank, N.A., Barclays Capital Real Estate Inc. and Wells Fargo Bank, National Association. KeyBank National Association will serve as master servicer, and Situs Holdings, LLC will serve as special servicer. Deutsche Bank National Trust Company will act as trustee while Computershare Trust Company, N.A. will act as certificate administrator. Pentalpha Surveillance LLC will act as operating advisor.

The certificates are expected to follow a sequential-pay structure.

KEY RATING DRIVERS

Fitch Net Cash Flow: Fitch's stressed net cash flow (NCF) for the property is estimated at $75.6 million; this is 13.5% lower than the issuer's NCF.

High Fitch Stressed Leverage: Fitch's stressed loan-to-value ratio (LTV) and debt yield (DY) for the total $970.0 million of debt are 125.0% and 7.8%, respectively. The lowest Fitch-rated tranche (Class B) has a Fitch LTV and DY of 58.6% and 16.6%, respectively. Fitch decreased its LTV hurdles by 2.5% to reflect the higher in-place leverage. The Fitch leverage metrics are based on a stressed cap rate of 10.0% and an NCF of $75.6 million, resulting in a Fitch value of approximately $775.7 million. The mortgage loan represents approximately 57.0% of the property's as-is appraised value of $1.7 billion.

Strong Asset Quality with Beachfront Location: The property is a historic luxury beachfront resort, uniquely situated on approximately 30 acres of Coronado Island in California, with 1,400 feet of direct private beach access, which is a rarity for a large resort in Southern California. The property offers various types of luxury room accommodations within five distinct "neighborhoods," including The Views, The Cabanas, Victorian Building, Beach Village and the Shore House.

Property amenities include three pools, 13 food and beverage (F&B) outlets, 244,000 sf of indoor and outdoor event and meeting space, a fitness and spa center and 10 retail shops. The property is primarily a destination resort but also draws group and convention business; as such, it benefits from its proximity to downtown San Diego, located about five miles away. The property also benefits from high barriers to entry in Southern California. Fitch assigned Hotel Del Coronado a property quality grade of "A".

Substantial Recent Capital Investment: The property recently underwent an extensive repositioning, with about $569 million ($606,858 per key) in capital improvements made by the sponsor, Blackstone, since acquisition in late 2015. Investments include the recently completed full renovation of the Victorian Building at an estimated $159 million, which included updates to all guestrooms, the addition of 37 new keys, and renovation of the building's meeting and event spaces, as well as the 2022 $166 million development of the Southpointe Event Center conference space and Shore House, delivering 75 condominium units operating as 142 hotel keys. Other investments include a $43 million renovation of The Cabanas, which included updates to the sundeck and pool; and approximately $35 million allocated to a repositioning of F&B outlets, including the addition of high-end eateries Nobu Del Coronado and Veranda.

Institutional Sponsor: The sponsor is Blackstone Real Estate Partners VIII, an affiliate of Blackstone Inc., a leading global alternative asset manager with over $1.0 trillion in assets and the largest owner of CRE in the world, with $320 billion of investor capital under management. Blackstone owns over 1,000 hotels and resorts and more than 158,000 room keys globally across upscale, extended-stay and select-service property types.

RATING SENSITIVITIES Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Negative Rating Action/Downgrade

Declining cash flow decreases property value and capacity to meet its debt service obligations. The table below indicates the model-implied rating (MIR) sensitivity to changes in one variable, Fitch NCF:

--Original Rating: 'AAAsf' / AA-sf';

--10% NCF Decline: 'AAsf' / 'A-sf'.

Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Positive Rating Action/Upgrade

Improvement in cash flow increases property value and capacity to meet its debt service obligations. The table below indicates the MIR sensitivity to changes to in one variable, Fitch NCF:

--Original Rating: 'AAAsf' / AA-sf';

--10% NCF Increase: 'AAAsf' / 'AAsf'.

USE OF THIRD PARTY DUE DILIGENCE PURSUANT TO SEC RULE 17G -10

Fitch was provided with Form ABS Due Diligence-15E (Form 15E) as prepared by PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP. The third-party due diligence described in Form 15E focused on a comparison and re-computation of certain characteristics with respect to each of the mortgage loans. Fitch considered this information in its analysis and it did not have an effect on Fitch's analysis or conclusions.

REFERENCES FOR SUBSTANTIALLY MATERIAL SOURCE CITED AS KEY DRIVER OF RATING The principal sources of information used in the analysis are described in the Applicable Criteria. REPRESENTATIONS, WARRANTIES AND ENFORCEMENT MECHANISMS A description of the transaction's representations, warranties and enforcement mechanisms (RW&Es) that are disclosed in the offering document and which relate to the underlying asset pool is available by clicking the link to the Appendix. The appendix also contains a comparison of these RW&Es to those Fitch considers typical for the asset class as detailed in the Special Report titled 'Representations, Warranties and Enforcement Mechanisms in Global Structured Finance Transactions'.

ESG Considerations

The highest level of ESG credit relevance is a score of '3', unless otherwise disclosed in this section. A score of '3' means ESG issues are credit-neutral or have only a minimal credit impact on the entity, either due to their nature or the way in which they are being managed by the entity. Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores are not inputs in the rating process; they are an observation on the relevance and materiality of ESG factors in the rating decision. For more information on Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores, visit https://www.fitchratings.com/topics/esg/products#esg-relevance-scores.

data.title: Nvidia's A100 Chip Use Shows Demand Is Resilient — Market Talk
data.description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang gave a three-pronged explanation of why AI isn't in a bubble, but he didn't mention the fourth, possibly most compelling argument, Truist analysts led by William Stein say. Stein thinks the clearest evidence that AI isn't yet in a bubble is that Nvidia's A100 chips, which began shipping six years ago, are still around and running at 100% utilization. For Stein, the biggest indication of a bubble would be equipment that's been ordered or shipped but doesn't have economic or operational value. Because all of the chips are still being used, it seems Nvidia's demand is going strong, Stein says. (katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/molina-healthcare-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=178401&wire=1&utm_campaign=24

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against Molina Healthcare, Inc. that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between February 5, 2025 and July 23, 2025.

CASE DETAILS: According to the filed complaint, defendants made false statements and/or concealed: (1) material, adverse facts concerning the Company's "medical cost trend assumptions;" (2) that Molina was experiencing a "dislocation between premium rates and medical cost trend;" (3) that Molina's near term growth was dependent on a lack of "utilization of behavioral health, pharmacy, and inpatient and outpatient services;" (4) as a result of the foregoing, Molina's financial guidance for fiscal year 2025 was substantially likely to be cut; and (5) that, as a result of the foregoing, defendants' positive statements about the Company's business, operations, and prospects were materially misleading and/or lacked a reasonable basis

Molina's stock price dropped following this news.

WHAT'S NEXT? If you suffered a loss in Molina stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to https://zlk.com/pslra-1/molina-healthcare-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=178401&wire=1&utm_campaign=24 to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. There is no cost or obligation to participate.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

data.title: Toronto Stocks Slip, Led Lower by Miners
data.description:
By Robb M. Stewart

Shares on Canada's main exchange retreated Thursday, dragged lower my weakness in mining stocks.

After beginning the session in positive territory, the Toronto Stock Exchange's S&P/TSX Composite Index by midday was 0.8% lower, at 30052.85. The mining sector was the biggest drag, leading broad losses and offsetting a gain by the finance segment.

The blue-chip S&P/TSX 60 was 0.5% weaker, at 1773.52.

Among mining companies, Agnico Eagle was down 3.5%, Barrick Mining lost 3.9%, Hudbay Minerals shed 3.7% and Teck Resources shed 2.3%.

Other market movers:

Shares of Vizsla Silver dropped 13%, to 5.74 Canadian dollars (US$4.09), after the company priced a US$250 million offering of convertible notes, raising funds to develop its flagship project in Mexico and to prepare for possible future acquisitions.

Real Matters rose 1.5%, to C$6.11, after the company reported a slight increase in fourth-quarter revenue as growth in Canada and U.S. refinance-origination revenue offset lower activity in the U.S. purchase market.

Write to Robb M. Stewart at robb.stewart@wsj.com
